data.title: Bain Is Said To Near $3 Billion Deal For Mitsubishi Pharma Unit- Bloomberg News
data.description: • BAIN IS SAID TO NEAR $3 BILLION DEAL FOR MITSUBISHI PHARMA UNIT- BLOOMBERG NEWS

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0348 GMT - Investors should take a selective approach to the Malaysian technology sector, focusing on stocks with clear strategies, long-term vision, and earnings visibility through secured orders, says AmInvestment Bank Analyst Paul Yap Ee Xing in a note. Equipment makers offer the best risk-reward, supported by stable earnings forecasts, a recovering order book, and trends in supply chain relocation, he reckons. While high earnings expectations pose a risk, Yap still sees value in the sector, given low investor holdings and strong long-term growth potential. As AI and data center trades unwind, investors seeking to rebalance their portfolio may return, he adds. AmInvestment Bank maintains an overweight rating on Malaysia's technology sector and pegs Vitrox Corp. and VS Industry as its top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0345 GMT - SGH remains a buy for UBS analysts, who anticipate ongoing earnings momentum from the Australian engineering and construction group. They think that the stock's recent rerate to 20 times earnings on a one-year forward basis, compared with a 10-year average of 13X, has been underpinned by investors' greater appreciation of its proven earnings delivery and investment discipline. With operational discipline at the Boral business now under SGH's control and mining maintenance demand looking strong at its WesTrac unit, they forecast average annual earnings growth of 8% for the next three fiscal years. UBS raises its target price 24% to A$56.00. Shares are up 0.7% at A$49.32. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0314 GMT - Yangzijiang Shipbuilding's management is expected to guide for higher new order wins this year, says UOB Kay Hian analyst Adrian Loh. The shipbuilder's new Yangzi Hongyuan yard in China will be ready for operation in 2H 2026, he writes in a note. With the new capacity taken into consideration, the brokerage estimates Yangzijiang Shipbuilding's new order win target could be between US$6 billion and US$7 billion. The company has a 2024 order win target of US$4.5 billion. UOB Kay Hian has a buy rating on the stock and a target price of S$3.60. Shares are flat at S$3.07.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0250 GMT - Keppel Infrastructure Trust's acquisition of a 50% equity interest in Marina East Water looks positive, OCBC Investment Research's Ada Lim says in a note. The bank maintains the trust's buy rating. The acquisition entitles the trust to full economic benefit from MEW, which owns Keppel Marina East Desalination Plant. The acquisition is expected to be accretive to unitholders, as the plant is a high-quality asset that will generate long-term stable cash flows, Lim says. The nature of this asset is also aligned with the trust's portfolio and deepens exposure to water treatment in Singapore, she adds. However, OCBC trims the trust's fair value estimate to S$0.500 from S$0.510 to reflect factors including a higher risk-free rate. Units are unchanged at S$0.450. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0221 GMT - KLCC Property Holdings' profit is expected to improve this year, driven by stronger tourism in Malaysia and full-year earnings contribution from its additional 40% stake in shopping mall Suria KLCC, Hong Leong IB says. Malaysia's Asean chairmanship in 2025 and the Visit Malaysia 2026 campaign could boost tourist arrivals, benefiting its hotel assets and Suria KLCC footfall, analyst Jack Heng Shao Jun says in a note. However, higher finance costs from the Suria KLCC acquisition could cap earnings growth, he says. Hong Leong maintains a hold rating on the REIT and keeps the target price at MYR7.71. Units are 0.4% higher at MYR8.38. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0152 GMT - Chinese shares are higher in early trade. The Shanghai Composite Index is up 0.1%, the Shenzhen Composite Index is 0.2% higher and the ChiNext Price Index is up 0.1%. Investors are focusing on tariff-related developments. Among major stocks, banks are leading the gains, with Industrial & Commercial Bank of China adding 1.05% and Industrial Bank gaining 0.35%. Tech stocks are lower as investors take some profits after a significant daily gain on Wednesday. Beijing Kingsoft Office Software is down 2.7% and iFlytek is 1.5% lower.(tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0148 GMT - Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rises 0.3% to 20661.51, tracking Wall Street gains. Markets are likely stabilizing as tariff-related news took a breather, the UOB Global Economics & Markets Research team writes in a note. Lenovo Group rises 4.0%, Techtronic Industries gains 3.5% and Nongfu Spring adds 3.0%. Meanwhile, Baidu falls 1.4%, AIA Group loses 0.85% and WH Group is 0.6% lower. The Hang Seng Tech Index is 0.4% higher at 4950.88. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0105 GMT - Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index rises 0.4% to 3829.32, tracking Wall Street's gains overnight. Global equities mostly stabilized, with trade tariff-related news taking a breather, says UOB's Global Economics & Markets Research team in a note. Singapore Exchange rises 1.1% after its 1H results. Other advancers include Thai Beverage, which is up 1.7% and Keppel Ltd., which rises 1.2%. Meanwhile, Wilmar International falls 1.2% and Singtel sheds 0.6%.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0104 GMT - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index is 0.1% higher at 1576.42. Improved global sentiment after the tariff pause for Mexico and Canada may sustain bargain hunting on the local bourse, though volatility keeps investors cautious amid ongoing tariff risks and China's slowdown, Apex Securities says in a note. Data-center stocks could rebound after steep losses, while construction and utilities sectors may attract bargain hunting, the brokerage says. Tech stocks may track Nasdaq's overnight gains, while the energy sector may face profit-taking as oil prices drop on rising U.S. crude inventories, it adds. Among gainers, YTL Corp. adds 1.0% and YTL Power International is up 1.3%. Meanwhile, MISC Bhd. is down 1.6% and Petronas Gas is 1.0% lower.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0045 GMT - Droneshield's bull at Shaw & Partners thinks that the security tech provider's long-term growth prospects are intact despite softer-than-expected December-quarter revenue. Analyst Abraham Akra tells clients in a note that quarterly revenue fell well short of his forecast, prompting him to trim his 2025 and 2026 revenue expectations by 20%. This reflects delayed tender activity and a decline in global conflicts, he explains. Even so, he keeps a buy rating on Droneshield, which develops drone-detection hardware and software. Shaw has a A$0.90 target price on the stock, which is down 1.5% at A$0.65. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0026 GMT - Pinnacle's bull at Wilsons Advisory believes that its opportunity in Australia's managed-accounts market has yet to be fully priced into its stock. Analyst Cameron Halkett tells clients in a note that he has conviction in the medium-term EPS growth potential from managed accounts offered by Pinnacle affiliate Pacific Asset Management. He points to Life Cycle's strong performance since becoming an affiliate in Pinnacle's most recent fiscal half as evidence of the opportunity on offer. It's possible that Pinnacle could deliver more than A$100 million in gross performance fees in its fiscal second half, Halkett adds. Wilsons lifts its target price 9% to A$28.95 and stays overweight on the stock, which is down 1.7% at A$25.60. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

0025 GMT - Japanese stocks are higher as concerns about trade friction between the U.S. and its trading partners recede. Brokerage and electronics stocks are leading the gains. Nomura Holdings is up 6.6% after its nine-month net profit more than doubled. Renesas Electronics is 8.8% higher after fourth-quarter results. USD/JPY is at 152.63, compared with 153.27 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching developments in U.S. tariffs and domestic earnings. Itochu and Fujifilm Holdings are scheduled to report their quarterly earnings later in the day. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 0.9% at 39180.28. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0348 GMT - Investors should take a selective approach to the Malaysian technology sector, focusing on stocks with clear strategies, long-term vision, and earnings visibility through secured orders, says AmInvestment Bank Analyst Paul Yap Ee Xing in a note. Equipment makers offer the best risk-reward, supported by stable earnings forecasts, a recovering order book, and trends in supply chain relocation, he reckons. While high earnings expectations pose a risk, Yap still sees value in the sector, given low investor holdings and strong long-term growth potential. As AI and data center trades unwind, investors seeking to rebalance their portfolio may return, he adds. AmInvestment Bank maintains an overweight rating on Malaysia's technology sector and pegs Vitrox Corp. and VS Industry as its top picks. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0314 GMT - Yangzijiang Shipbuilding's management is expected to guide for higher new order wins this year, says UOB Kay Hian analyst Adrian Loh. The shipbuilder's new Yangzi Hongyuan yard in China will be ready for operation in 2H 2026, he writes in a note. With the new capacity taken into consideration, the brokerage estimates Yangzijiang Shipbuilding's new order win target could be between US$6 billion and US$7 billion. The company has a 2024 order win target of US$4.5 billion. UOB Kay Hian has a buy rating on the stock and a target price of S$3.60. Shares are flat at S$3.07.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0250 GMT - Keppel Infrastructure Trust's acquisition of a 50% equity interest in Marina East Water looks positive, OCBC Investment Research's Ada Lim says in a note. The bank maintains the trust's buy rating. The acquisition entitles the trust to full economic benefit from MEW, which owns Keppel Marina East Desalination Plant. The acquisition is expected to be accretive to unitholders, as the plant is a high-quality asset that will generate long-term stable cash flows, Lim says. The nature of this asset is also aligned with the trust's portfolio and deepens exposure to water treatment in Singapore, she adds. However, OCBC trims the trust's fair value estimate to S$0.500 from S$0.510 to reflect factors including a higher risk-free rate. Units are unchanged at S$0.450. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0245 GMT - Palm oil prices rise in early Asian trading, tracking the gains in soybean oil on the Chicago Board of Trade, says David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg X. Expectations of weaker palm oil output further support CPO prices, he adds. Malaysia's January palm oil output is expected to have declined 10% from the previous month to 1.34 million tons, a Bloomberg survey shows. Ng pegs CPO support at 4,300 ringgit/ton and resistance at 4,450 ringgit/ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for April delivery is 9 ringgit higher at 4,343 ringgit/ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0133 GMT - Oil edges higher in early Asian trade, after falling overnight as data showed a weekly climb of almost 9 million barrels in U.S. crude inventories. Crude oil prices may continue to be weighed by rising U.S. stockpiles and trade-related uncertainty, says Tickmill's Joseph Dahrieh in a note. This reinforces a bearish sentiment due to persistent demand-side risks, the managing principal writes. Although supply constraints would provide some stability, expectations of rising production in the U.S. and from OPEC members could weigh on the market over the longer-term, he says. Front-month WTI crude oil futures rise 0.2% to $71.20/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are up 0.1% to $74.71/bbl.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

2312 GMT - Comet Ridge's signal that a final investment decision on its Mahalo natural-gas project in Australia won't happen this year contributes to Bell Potter cutting its price target by 19% to A$0.21/share. Still, analyst Stuart Howe remains bullish about Comet Ridge, highlighting that Mahalo is one of the few development-ready projects capable of delivering near-term gas supply to eastern Australia. Comet Ridge expects preliminary design and engineering work at Mahalo to continue through this year. Only when this is completed will it make a final investment decision, or FID, with partner Santos. "We expect Comet Ridge's majority equity positions in the Mahalo projects and a strong gas market outlook to provide support for development funding across debt, equity and gas prepayments," Bell Potter says. "However, we also acknowledge risks to FID timing." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2211 GMT - Canadian Trade Minister Mary Ng says Ottawa is ready to enter talks with Trump administration officials on "priority" bilateral trade issues with a goal of avoiding a 25% tariff in early March. Ng provides no concrete examples on what the Liberal government might do, during an interview with Canada's CTV News. Ng, though, says Ottawa won't make concessions on agriculture, especially measures aimed at protecting Canada's dairy producers. Canada's system of protecting dairy farmers was a point of attack for President Trump's nominee for Commerce Secretary, Howard Lutnick, during a confirmation hearing last week. (paul.vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

2244 GMT - Beach Energy's interim dividend falls short of market expectations, with Barrenjoey noting the payout doesn't align with the company's distribution policy. Beach declared an interim dividend of 3 Australian cents a share. While that was up 50% on last year it fell short of the 4 cents anticipated by the market. Analyst Dale Koenders points out that Beach reported free cash flow of A$431 million in 1H. "But this free cash flow then also implies a payout ratio of only 16%, below policy of 40-50%," Koenders says in his initial response to the result. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1940 GMT - Oil futures fall as the EIA reports a bigger-then-expected 8.7 million barrel increase in U.S. crude inventories. "Tariff developments have buffeted the entire energy complex with potential additional restraints on Iranian crude exports adding to geopolitical uncertainties," Ritterbusch says in a note. "But with the tariffs on Canada and Mexico likely pushed to the sidelines for a couple of weeks, the oil market will be able to zero in more clearly on underlying fundamentals." U.S. gasoline stocks extended their buildup to 12 weeks, while distillate stocks fell by a hefty 5.5 million barrels. "We continue to view gasoline as the weak leg in the complex," Ritterbusch adds. WTI settles down 2.3% at $71.03 a barrel, and Brent falls 2.1% to $74.61. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1917 GMT - U.S. natural gas futures pick up ahead of tomorrow's weekly inventory report that's expected to leave the storage deficit against the five-year average largely intact. Analysts in a Wall Street Journal survey predict a withdrawal of 170 Bcf to 2,401 Bcf, which would be 107 Bcf below the five-year average and 204 Bcf less than a year ago. Temperature forecasts and supply and demand fundamentals were little changed, and LNG feedgas nominations "look to be in good shape with a 15.1 Bcf/d flow," Gelber & Associates says in a note. The Nymex front month settles up 3.3% at $3.360/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1845 GMT - A third large weekly inventory draw leaves U.S. distillate stocks 12% below their five-year average as a colder-than-expected January drove extra demand for heating fuel. The inventory decline "is not too surprising given where refineries are operating, still below 85% of operable capacity, but that is something that creates near-term price risk for diesel," says Matt Muenster, chief economist at transport technology firm Breakthrough. Monday's pause in threatened tariffs on Canadian oil took some pressure off prices, and although the cost would likely be shared among Canadian oil producers, U.S. refiners and consumers, "if they had stayed, that ultimately was going to get passed to transportation pretty quickly," he adds. Also offsetting the price impact of lower diesel stocks is the seasonal build in crude oil inventories, Muenster says. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1733 GMT - Global sustainable bond issuance is expected to remain steady in 2025, at about $1 trillion, according to S&P Global Ratings in a report. The agency says "with green bonds and supranational entities in the lead, the sustainable bond market seems to have returned to its roots." Corporate green bond issuance is now well established, S&P says. However, issuance fell in 2024 to about 11% of the global total, from 13% in 2023. More issuance in low-income and emerging economies is expected to address the climate finance gap, S&P says, but adds "we see a risk that macroeconomic and political factors could stifle further growth." (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

data.title: Hanssem Narrows Loss in Q4 2024
data.description:
Hanssem narrowed the fourth-quarter 2024 net attributable loss to shareholders of the parent to 5.12 billion won from 34.1 billion won a year earlier.

The South Korean furniture company's sales fell 2.3% year over year to 490.4 billion won from 502.0 billion won, according to a Wednesday Korea Exchange filing.

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 0.3% Higher at 78513.36, Led by Tech Stocks
BCPG Target Price Lowered to THB9.00 From THB10.00 by Thanachart Securities >BCPG.TH
Belite Bio Enters Securities Purchase Agreement With a New Investor for Purchase and Sale of 258,309 ADSs at $58.07 Per ADS >BLTE
Over 1.9 Billion Parcels Moved Across China During Spring Festival Holiday
data.description:
Over 1.9 billion parcels were moved across China during the eight-day Spring Festival holiday ending on Tuesday, Feb. 4, Xinhua reported Wednesday, citing the State Post Bureau.

The Chinese postal and courier industry has collected over 8 billion parcel since Jan. 14, when the festival rush started, a 38% rise over the year.

Meanwhile, around 8.65 billion deliveries were made around the same time, a 35% increase from last year, the publication said.

data.title: Belite Bio: Offering Expected to Result in Gross Proceeds of Approximately $15M >BLTE
Belite Bio: Closing of Offering Expected to Occur on or About Feb. 7 >BLTE
Helmerich and Payne Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Helmerich and Payne Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 71 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 97 cents. The mean expectation of fifteen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 68 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 42 cents to 85 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 0% to $677.30 million from a year ago; analysts expected $719.38 million.

  • Helmerich and Payne Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 54 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $54.77 million.

  • Helmerich and Payne Inc shares had risen by 1.8% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 16.5% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, two analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 7 "strong buy" or "buy," 10 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the oil & gas drilling peer group is "hold."

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Helmerich and Payne Inc is $40.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 6 at 03:42 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Thailand Inflation Rate Slightly Above Expectations
data.description:
Thailand's annual inflation rate rose to 1.32% in January 2025, up from 1.23% in the previous month and slightly above the expected 1.30%.

This marks the highest reading since May 2024 and the second consecutive period within the central bank’s target range of 1% to 3%.

data.title: Thailand's Inflation Picked Up in January
data.description:
By Amanda Lee

Thailand's inflation sped up in the first month of the year, staying at the lower end of the central bank's target range for the second month in a row.

Headline inflation was at 1.32% last month compared with the same period a year earlier, government data showed Thursday. That was up from December's 1.23% reading, and compares with the 1.3% forecast in a Wall Street Journal survey of seven economists.

The Bank of Thailand has an inflation target of 1.0% to 3.0%.

Core inflation, which excludes private road transport and accommodation costs, was 0.83% in January, up from December's 0.79%.

January's print suggests that the Southeast Asian economy is gradually improving, potentially easing pressure on the central bank to cut interest rates.

Thailand has been grappling with persistently low inflation through most of 2024. In December, the headline reading broke into the lower end of the BOT's target range for the first time after six months of languishing below the threshold.

The central bank made its first rate cut in over four years in October to help ease debt-servicing burdens.

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

data.title: SGH Bulls Anticipate Ongoing Earnings Momentum — Market Talk
data.description: SGH remains a buy for UBS analysts, who anticipate ongoing earnings momentum from the Australian engineering and construction group. They think that the stock's recent rerate to 20 times earnings on a one-year forward basis, compared with a 10-year average of 13X, has been underpinned by investors' greater appreciation of its proven earnings delivery and investment discipline. With operational discipline at the Boral business now under SGH's control and mining maintenance demand looking strong at its WesTrac unit, they forecast average annual earnings growth of 8% for the next three fiscal years. UBS raises its target price 24% to A$56.00. Shares are up 0.7% at A$49.32. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Tether is building AI apps and an open-source SDK platform
data.description:
The world’s largest stablecoin issuer, Tether, is venturing deeper into artificial intelligence with a number of AI applications in development, according to the firm’s chief executive.

Tether’s AI division, Tether Data, is developing a number of AI apps, such as AI Translate, AI Voice Assistant, and AI Bitcoin Wallet Assistant, according to a Feb. 4 announcement from CEO Paolo Ardoino.

Paolo Ardoino 🤖🍐
@paoloardoino

Tether Data, preview of some of the AI apps we're developing: AI translate, AI voice assistant, AI bitcoin wallet assistant.

Tether will launch soon its own AI SDK platform, open-source, built upon Bare (Holepunch's javascript runtime), working on every hardware, from embedded… pic.twitter.com/W5JFmoVcnh

Feb 04, 2025

“Tether will soon launch its own AI SDK [software development kit] platform, open-source, built upon Bare, working on every hardware, from embedded devices, any mobile phone, any laptop, powerful servers, clusters of servers,” he added. 

Further details were thin but Ardoino said that Tether Data’s apps “will focus on working locally on any device, full privacy, self-custodial for both data and money.”

In the demo for the AI Bitcoin Wallet Assistant, a user asked the “Payment Agent” what their BTC wallet address was before querying about the BTC balance. 

The demo also showed the user asking what recipients they had in their address book before requesting the agent send a small amount of BTC to one of them.

The entire process was conducted through the AI chatbot interface and carried out autonomously. 

Tether’s AI Translate was a simple AI chatbot translation tool and AI Voice Assistant involved the chatbot responding to voice inputs rather than text. 

Tether’s AI ambitions were evident back in 2023 when the firm acquired a stake in European crypto miner Northern Data Group, which specializes in cloud computing and generative AI. 

In March, Tether further expanded its AI operations with a global recruitment drive for top-tier talent, telling Cointelegraph at the time that it “plans to push the boundaries of AI technology,” and set “new industry standards for innovation and utility.”

In December, Ardoino said he “just got the draft of the site for Tether’s AI platform,” before adding that the firm was targeting a launch at the end of Q1, 2025. 

On Jan. 31, Tether announced record-breaking profits of $13 billion in 2024 and revealed having a larger-than-ever stockpile of US government bonds. Tether’s US Treasury portfolio is now worth approximately $113 billion, it stated. 

Meanwhile, its stablecoin (USDT) is the third-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, which is at an all-time high of $141 billion, following the issuance of another billion dollars worth on Feb. 5.

data.title: Iron ore rebounds on Australia supply worries, softer dollar
data.description:
Iron ore futures rebounded on Thursday, supported by a softer dollar and Australia supply worries, while investors looked for fresh developments surrounding the trade war between the United States and top consumer China.

The most-traded May iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) rose 1.12% to 815 yuan ($111.92) a metric ton by 0311 GMT, after closing lower on Wednesday.

The benchmark March iron ore (SZZFH5) on the Singapore Exchange was 1.74% higher at $105.6 a ton.

Australia is in its hurricane season, and there is a risk of disruption in shipments, Chinese consultancy Hexun Futures said in a note.

Rio Tinto , said on Tuesday it had begun clearing iron ore ships from two Western Australian ports as two tropical cyclones complicated its efforts to repair infrastructure damaged by a previous cyclone last month.

Rio's port struggles could add a risk premium to iron ore prices, said an analyst.

The Western Australia cyclone season typically occurs between November and April.

Also providing some support to prices was a weaker U.S dollar , which slumped to an eight-week trough to the yen and lingered near a one-month low versus the sterling.

A weaker dollar makes greenback-denominated commodities more affordable for overseas buyers.

China filed a World Trade Organization complaint on Wednesday against U.S. President Donald Trump's new 10% tariff on Chinese imports and his cancellation of the "de minimis" exemption.

Meanwhile, the Chinese foreign ministry urged for dialogue between the two countries.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE advanced, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) up 1.44% and 0.87%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange gained ground. Both rebar and wire rod (SWRcv1) were up nearly 0.4%, hot-rolled coil rose 0.5%, and stainless steel added 0.6%.

($1 = 7.2818 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Parkway Life Industrial REIT Logs Lower DPU in H2 2024
data.description:
Parkway Life Industrial REIT's distribution per unit declined to SG$0.0738 in the second half of 2024 from SG$0.0748 a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

Amount available for distribution to unitholders rose 2.3% to SG$92.4 million from SG$89.3 million in the comparable period.

Net property income declined 1.1% to SG$68.2 million from SG$69.0 million a year earlier.

Gross revenue was down 0.3% year over year to SG$72.8 million from SG$73.1 million, largely due to the depreciation of the Japanese yen.

data.title: Taiwan's January Inflation Likely Gained Pace Due to Holiday — Market Talk
data.description: Taiwan's consumer prices likely rose 2.4% on year in January, according to the median forecast of six estimates polled by The Wall Street Journal. That compares with the 2.1% inflation print in December, and 2.08% in November. Economists think that inflation likely accelerated due to the Lunar New Year holiday falling in January this year against early February in 2024. Prices of food and services were notably higher during the holiday period. Looking ahead, DBS economists expect inflation to remain stable, driven by weak oil prices and a cooling property market. However, possible U.S. tariffs on oil, gas, semiconductors, among others, could weigh on consumer prices, they caution. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: TPK Unit Sells Shares in HD Renewable Energy for Over NT$336 Million
data.description:
TPK unit TPK Touch Solutions sold 1.5 million shares of HD Renewable Energy on the centralized exchange for NT$336.4 million.

The shares were sold from Aug. 20, 2024, to Feb. 5, 2025, at an average price of NT$228.2 per share, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The sale will lead in a gain of NT$217.1 million, and disposal was made to realize capital gains, the electronic component manufacturer said.

data.title: CTBC Financial Unit Buys CM TMK-Issued Bonds for 200 Million Yen
data.description:
CTBC Financial unit The Tokyo Star Bank acquired corporate bonds issued by CM TMK worth 200 million yen.

The investment represents 0.204% of total assets and 3.144% of shareholders' equity, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Thai January headline CPI rises 1.32% y/y, in line with forecast
data.description:
Thailand's headline consumer price index (THCPI=ECI) rose 1.32% in January from a year earlier, after the previous month's annual increase of 1.23%, the commerce ministry said on Thursday.

The figure compared with a forecast rise of 1.30% in a Reuters poll and was inside the central bank's target range of 1% to 3% for the second consecutive month.

The core CPI (THCPIX=ECI) was up 0.83% in January from a year earlier, versus a forecast increase of 0.80%.

data.title: Tenable Holdings Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Tenable Holdings Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 41 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 25 cents. The mean expectation of twenty analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 34 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 34 cents to 35 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 10.5% to $235.73 million from a year ago; analysts expected $231.60 million.

  • Tenable Holdings Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 2 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $1.87 million.

data.title: Delta Electronics Unit to Buy Pre-Sale Townhouses in Texas for $12 Million
data.description:
Delta Electronics' unit Delta Electronics (USA) plans to acquire 21 pre-sale townhouses in Plano, Texas, valued at $12 million.

The properties, spanning 49,749 square feet, will be purchased from Ashton Dallas Residential, according to a Wednesday Taiean Exchange filing.

The properties will support business development of the company, it said.

data.title: Belite Bio Announces Registered Direct Offering of $15 Million
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, Feb. 05, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Belite Bio, Inc ("BLTE" or the "Company") , a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical drug development company focused on advancing novel therapeutics targeting degenerative retinal diseases that have significant unmet medical needs, today announced that it has entered into a securities purchase agreement with a new, fundamental healthcare investor for the purchase and sale of 258,309 American Depositary Shares (“ADSs”) and warrants to purchase 258,309 ADSs, at a purchase price of $58.07 per ADS and accompanying warrant, pursuant to a registered direct offering, equivalent to today’s closing price. The offering is expected to result in gross proceeds of approximately $15 million, before deducting offering expenses, as well as the potential for additional proceeds of approximately $15 million from the exercise of five-year warrants issued in the offering. The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about February 7, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

The Company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.

Titan Partners Group, a division of American Capital Partners, is acting as the sole placement agent for the offering.

This offering is being made pursuant to a shelf registration statement on Form F-3 (File No. 333-284521) previously filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the “SEC”) and declared effective on January 27, 2025. The offering is made only by means of a prospectus supplement, which will be filed with the SEC and will be available on the SEC’s website located at www.sec.gov. Electronic copies of the prospectus supplement may be obtained, when available, by contacting Titan Partners Group LLC, a division of American Capital Partners, LLC, 4 World Trade Center, 29th Floor, New York, NY 10007, by phone at (929) 833-1246 or by email at prospectus@titanpartnersgrp.com.

This press release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy nor shall there be any sale of these securities in any state or jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or jurisdiction.

About Belite Bio

Belite Bio, Inc is a clinical stage biopharmaceutical drug development company focused on advancing novel therapeutics targeting retinal degenerative eye diseases with significant unmet medical needs, such as Stargardt disease and Geographic Atrophy in advanced dry age-related macular degeneration, in addition to specific metabolic diseases.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements, including statements about future expectations, plans and prospects, as well as any statements regarding matters that are not historical facts, and any other statements containing the words “expect”, “will”, “believe”, “target”, and other similar expressions. No assurance can be given that the offering will be completed on the terms described. Completion of the offering and the terms thereof are subject to numerous factors, many of which are beyond the control of BLTE, including, without limitation, market conditions, failure of customary closing conditions and the risk factors and other matters set forth in the prospectus supplement and accompanying prospectus included in the registration statement. Actual results may also differ materially from those indicated in the forward-looking statements as a result of various important factors related to BLTE’s business, including but not limited to BLTE’s ability to demonstrate the safety and efficacy of its drug candidates; the clinical results for its drug candidates, which may not support further development or regulatory approval; expectations for the timing of initiation, enrollment and completion of, and data relating to, its clinical trials; the content and timing of decisions made by the relevant regulatory authorities regarding regulatory approval of BLTE’s drug candidates; the potential efficacy of Tinlarebant, as well as those risks more fully discussed in the “Risk Factors” section in BLTE’s filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. All forward-looking statements are based on information currently available to BLTE, and BLTE undertakes no obligation to publicly update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as may be required by law.

Media and Investor Relations Contact:

Jennifer Wu / ir@belitebio.com

Julie Fallon / belite@argotpartners.com

data.title: USPS Reverses Ban on Chinese, Hong Kong Mail Despite Tariffs
data.description:
The US Postal Service said it will continue accepting international inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong, reversing a previously announced supsension, the USPS said in a notice on Wednesday.

Earlier this week, the USPS indicated it would suspend all Chinese parcels, subjecting them to formal customs entry. The policy shift came after the introduction of a 10% tariff on Chinese goods.

The USPS said it will now work with the Customs and Border Protection to implement an efficient collection mechanism for the new tariffs to mitigate disruption in delivering the packages.

data.title: BoJ Tamura Calls for Faster Rate Hikes
data.description:
Bank of Japan should raise interest rates to 1% or higher in the fiscal half that starts in October 2025, board member Naoki Tamura said in a speech. "Raising short-term interest rates to at least around 1% in the latter half of fiscal 2025 is necessary to reduce upside risk to prices and achieve the price stability target in a sustainable and stable manner," he said.

Tamura, a former executive at Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, is known for his hawkish stance.

In December, he advocated for a rate hike, citing increased risks to price stability.

Although his proposal was voted down, the policy board ultimately raised interest rates to 0.5% from 0.25% in their January meeting.

data.title: US Treas Secretary Scott Bessent - more on Trump wanting lower 10 yr yeild, not short term
data.description:
I posted earlier on Bessent:

  • Trump's Treasury Secretary Bessent threatens the largest tax hike in history

The key point in his comments was this:

  • focus is on bringing down 10-year Treasury yields, rather than the Fed’s benchmark short-term interest rate

Over the weekend Trump said it was 'fine' that the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) didn't cut rates last week. Which was a bit of a surprise given his demands and rants that they do so. If the focus really is on the 10-year then perhaps Trump's 'fine' makes sense.

Of course, getting the 10 year yield on a downward path implies moves to improve the US fiscal position, as well as inflation. So far we've had his partner, Musk, cutting Federal government programs like USAID, Federal employees and such. Which really doesn't scratch the surface, most of the US spending is on healthcare, Social Security, and defence. Will Trump inflict the pain that his focus seems to imply? There is a barely a politician out there that would.
